keyPoints: A component is a modular, deployable, and replaceable part of a system that encapsulates its contents and exposes its functionality through interfaces.
Components are connected through their provided and required interfaces to form a larger system.
Component-Based Development (CBD) focuses on building systems by assembling pre-built components, promoting reuse and reducing development time.
commonMistakes: Confusing a component with a simple code library or module; a component is a higher-level, independently deployable unit.
Assuming tight coupling is acceptable; well-designed components are loosely coupled and communicate strictly through defined interfaces.
Overlooking non-functional requirements (like performance or security) when selecting and integrating third-party components.
